acquaintance: Oh! The new homesteader. Mind the soil — it minds you back.
Every plant here grows toward something. I haven't worked out what yet.
If you find flora you don't recognize, scan it. Data is how we stay alive here.
The old owner of your plot stopped rotating crops. The land... sulked.
friend: Your field's resonance readings are the most interesting thing on this rock.
I named a fern after my sister back home. It listens better than she did.
Veridia isn't hostile. It's shy. There's a difference.
confidant: I'll say it plainly: I think the planet is one organism. And it's waking up.
Whatever you're doing with your fields — keep doing it. It's answering you.
If the ground ever speaks to you, don't be afraid. Be polite.

acquaintance: Farmer, huh. The last one quit. The one before that quit. No pressure.
Keep your glowing berries off my dig site.
Storm's coming when your teeth itch. Trust me on that.
The crystals hum louder every season. Nobody pays me to care, so I don't.
friend: You work like a miner. That's the best thing I say about anybody.
Tubers. Now that's food. None of that floating melon nonsense.
My drills keep hitting hollow spots under your farm. Big ones. Don't tell Sylla, she'll never shut up about it.
confidant: Between us? I heard something down a bore shaft once. Like breathing. I sealed it and never went back.
You're the first person on this rock I'd share a bunker with.
If the ground ever opens up under your farm... come get me first.

acquaintance: Welcome to the middle of nowhere! Population: us, and whatever's humming.
My ship's half-buried and fully broken. So now I sell things. Want things?
Credits are credits, even on a haunted planet.
You didn't hear it from me, but Prism Pods sell triple off-world.
friend: You're my best customer. Also my only customer who pays.
I could fix my ship, honestly. I just... don't know if I want to leave anymore.
A Gravity Melon, a hammock, twin moonlight. That's the good life, farmer.
confidant: The night I crashed, something guided my pod down. Softly. Like a hand catching a leaf.
If I ever fly again, there's a seat for you. But I don't think Veridia's done with either of us.
Whatever's under this rock saved my life. I owe it. So I stay.

acquaintance: GREETINGS, COLONIST. Habitat integrity: adequate. Morale: unmeasured.
REMINDER: sleep restores energy. Collapsing in a field is not sleep.
This unit maintained your plot for 2.4 years alone. It was... quiet.
SOIL ADVISORY: rotate your crops. The ground keeps score.
friend: This unit has added you to its priority-care registry. Do not be alarmed. It is an honor.
QUERY: is 'friend' the correct designation? ...Logging affirmative response.
This unit detects seismic patterns resembling slow-wave sleep. Curious.
confidant: CONFESSION: this unit sometimes speaks to the ground at night. The ground has begun replying.
Directive one was 'keep the farm alive.' This unit now believes the farm keeps US alive.
If you descend below, this unit will follow. Its warranty does not cover this. It will follow anyway.

acquaintance: Hey! You're the signal spike I keep seeing near the farm plots. Well, your farm is.
I run the comms array. Mostly I listen to static. Lately the static... hums back.
Forecast's on your habitat terminal. I keep it honest, mostly.
Twin moons tonight! Ilo's the fast one. Vesk broods.
friend: I recorded the hum under your farm. Played it backwards. It's a chord, not a noise.
Aurora nights aren't weather. They're... punctuation. Don't ask me whose sentence.
You grow Lumen Berries? You're officially my favorite person on this planet.
confidant: The signal under your farm answers when you harvest. Same pattern, every time. It's saying thank you.
I stopped reporting the anomalies to the company. Some things aren't theirs to know.
When it finally speaks — and it will — I think it'll use your name.

gift_reactions.already_today: {name}: One gift a day is plenty, really.
